query: Iran IAEA cooperation law suspension 2025 2026, current state of Iran nuclear inspections and diplomacy, US Iran nuclear deal negotiations status June July 2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on Iran-IAEA cooperation and US-Iran nuclear diplomacy as of early July 2026:

**IAEA Cooperation Suspension:**
- Iran's law suspending IAEA cooperation was enacted July 2, 2025, after the June 2025 strikes. A brief Cairo Agreement (Sept 2025) was terminated by Iran on Nov 20, 2025.
- Verification is effectively frozen—the IAEA reports it cannot determine the size, composition, or location of Iran's enriched uranium stockpile, representing a **complete loss of continuity of knowledge** (HEU unverified for ~1 year).
- Only Bushehr and the Tehran reactor are currently accessible; Fordow, Natanz, and Isfahan (all bombed) are not.

**Diplomatic Status:**
- After the dramatic Feb 28, 2026 US-Israel strikes (which killed Supreme Leader Khamenei), the US and Iran signed the **Islamabad Memorandum on June 17, 2026**, launching a 60-day window for a final deal.
- Two rounds of talks—Lucerne (June 21-22) and Doha (June 30-July 2)—yielded a communication channel and a $6bn frozen-funds agreement, but **no fundamental breakthrough**.
- The central unresolved dispute is **IAEA access**: Grossi insists inspections will resume "very soon" per the MoU, while Iran maintains access will only be resolved within a final agreement (its parliamentary law still bars broader inspections).
- Next direct talks are expected in Doha in the third week of July 2026.

Note: AskNews tools were unavailable (credit limit reached), so this relied on web search and direct source fetches from Al Jazeera, Arms Control Association, ISIS, CNBC, NPR, and Wikipedia. The full synthesis with all data points and sources has been submitted via the structured output.

sources.title: Iran and Nuclear Opacity: Strategic Ambiguity, Retaliation, and Leverage
sources.snippet: On July 2, 2025, President Pezeshkian formally enacted a law suspending Iran's cooperation with the IAEA, approved unanimously by parliament and endorsed by the Guardian Council.

sources.title: 2025-2026 Iran-United States negotiations
sources.snippet: Islamabad Memorandum signed June 17, 2026; Feb 28, 2026 strikes killed Khamenei and Larijani; timeline of negotiation rounds.

sources.title: Fighting Mars U.S.-Iranian Pledges to End War, Restart Nuclear Talks
sources.snippet: Details of the Islamabad Memorandum: down-blending under IAEA supervision, 60-day timeline, 15-year enrichment suspension proposal, sanctions relief.

sources.title: US-Iran talks in Doha: What were the outcomes and what's next?
sources.snippet: Doha talks concluded July 2, 2026; $6bn frozen funds agreement; IAEA access to only Bushehr and Tehran reactor; no access to Fordow, Natanz, Isfahan.

sources.title: Analysis of IAEA Iran Verification and Monitoring and NPT Safeguards Reports - June 2026
sources.snippet: IAEA cannot provide info on stockpile; no verification since Feb 28, 2026; complete loss of continuity of knowledge; Cairo Agreement terminated Nov 20, 2025.

sources.title: UN nuclear chief says Iran inspections will happen, Tehran says after deal
sources.snippet: Grossi says MoU explicitly provides for IAEA-supervised inspections; Iran says only after a final deal.

sources.title: Dispute widens over Iran nuclear inspections
sources.snippet: UN nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran, but Iran says only after final deal.

sources.title: US, Iran expected to hold direct talks in Doha in 3rd week of July
sources.snippet: Next direct US-Iran talks expected in Doha in third week of July 2026 to advance Islamabad Memorandum implementation.

sources.title: Nuclear inspectors and frozen assets: What Iran and US can't agree on
sources.snippet: US waived sanctions on Iranian oil for 60 days after first day of Switzerland talks; conflicting accounts of what was agreed.

sources.title: U.S.-Iran peace deal grants access to Tehran's nuclear sites, UN watchdog says
sources.snippet: IAEA head says interim peace agreement gives inspectors access to nuclear sites, despite Iran insisting no new plans for inspections.

sources.title: Iran to Suspend IAEA Deal After UN Sanctions Return
sources.snippet: Iran said it would suspend cooperation with the IAEA after the UN Security Council reimposed sanctions.
